text:
PET-CT SCAN WITH 18F-FDG
Whole Body (Skull Base to Mid-Thigh)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Marcus R. Ellison, 57-year-old male. Known BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma (Stage IV, M1c), primary site upper back. History of bilateral axillary and left cervical nodal involvement, multiple hepatic metastases, and widespread cutaneous/subcutaneous satellite nodules at diagnosis. Prior treatment: dabrafenib/trametinib combination (first-line) with initial excellent response but radiographic and clinical progression at approximately 8 months, consistent with acquired resistance. Subsequently transitioned to ipilimumab/nivolumab combination immunotherapy; course complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis requiring ipilimumab discontinuation, IV methylprednisolone, and infliximab. Resumed nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks as single-agent maintenance. Most recent cross-sectional restaging CT (approximately 9 months into immunotherapy) demonstrated deepening partial response with marked interval reduction of target lesions (right axillary mass 0.9 cm, hepatic segment VII lesion 0.6 cm, hepatic segment II lesion 0.4 cm) and resolution of previously identified subcutaneous nodules and small hepatic lesions. This PET-CT obtained at approximately 12 months from immunotherapy initiation for comprehensive metabolic response assessment. Concurrent hypothyroidism on levothyroxine 75 mcg/day (immune-mediated, stable). LDH 148 U/L (normal).

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body PET-CT acquired from skull base to mid-thigh following intravenous administration of 12.6 mCi of 18F-fluorodeoxyglucose (18F-FDG). Patient fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to injection. Blood glucose at time of FDG injection: 94 mg/dL. Uptake time: 60 minutes. CT component performed with intravenous contrast (100 mL iohexol, portal venous phase) and low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. Images reconstructed with iterative reconstruction algorithm. SUV measurements are lean body mass-corrected (SUVbw used for all reported values).

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
The brain demonstrates normal physiologic cortical and subcortical FDG distribution. No focal intracranial hypermetabolic lesions identified. The basal ganglia and cerebellum show expected symmetric baseline activity. No leptomeningeal FDG-avid foci are seen. Oral mucosa demonstrates typical physiologic activity.

In the neck, previously enlarged left cervical level II and III lymph nodes (measuring up to 2.2 cm at baseline staging and hypermetabolic with SUVmax 6.8-9.2 on prior PET-CT) are no longer visible as discrete enlarged nodes. Residual small lymph nodes in the left level II region measure up to 5 mm and show no FDG activity above background (SUVmax at this region 1.4, background). No new cervical, supraclavicular, or submandibular hypermetabolic foci.

CHEST:
Lungs: The lung parenchyma is clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidations. No pleural effusions. No pleural nodularity or enhancement. Mediastinum is unremarkable. Mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are normal in size and show no pathologic FDG uptake. Esophagus: normal physiologic mucosal activity.

Right axilla: There is a small residual right axillary soft tissue density measuring approximately 0.8 x 0.6 cm in the location corresponding to the previously bulky conglomerate right axillary nodal mass (which measured 4.8 x 3.9 cm at initial staging and carried an SUVmax of 18.7 on baseline PET-CT). The residual soft tissue focus demonstrates only faint, minimally elevated FDG activity (SUVmax 2.1), which is at or only marginally above the expected level of mediastinal blood pool activity (SUVmax mediastinal blood pool reference: 2.0). This likely represents treated/fibrotic residual nodal tissue without convincing metabolically viable disease. No other right axillary soft tissue masses or FDG-avid foci. The left axilla demonstrates no residual adenopathy and no abnormal FDG uptake.

Primary site (upper back): The previously described primary melanoma site in the skin/subcutaneous soft tissue of the upper back shows no residual or recurrent FDG-avid lesion. The post-excisional soft tissue in this region demonstrates only background-level activity.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
Liver: The liver is normal in size. Hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous in attenuation. Previously identified six hepatic metastases, which at baseline carried an SUVmax up to 14.3 in the largest lesion (segment VII), show the following:
  - Hepatic segment VII: A small area of residual subtle hypodensity is present measuring approximately 5 mm, representing the treatment bed of the previously documented 3.1 cm metastasis (3.1 cm at diagnosis, 2.8 cm at progression on targeted therapy, subsequently 1.2 cm and then 0.6 cm on prior CT restaging). FDG activity in this region is faint and indistinguishable from adjacent normal hepatic parenchymal activity (SUVmax 1.8, below metabolically significant threshold and within the range of normal hepatic background).
  - Hepatic segment II: The area corresponding to the prior segment II metastasis (1.8 cm at diagnosis, subsequently 0.4 cm at last CT) shows no discernible residual lesion on CT and no focal FDG activity above background.
  - Hepatic segment IV: Previously documented new lesion arising at disease progression (1.3 cm), which subsequently decreased to 0.4 cm on prior immunotherapy restaging, shows no identifiable CT density abnormality and no FDG activity.
  - Hepatic segment VI: The previously noted lesion in this segment (0.9 cm at progression, below threshold on last CT) shows no abnormality on either CT or FDG imaging.
  - No new hepatic lesions are identified. The portal and hepatic venous structures are patent. No perihepatic fluid.

Biliary system: Normal. No intra- or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation.

Spleen: Normal in size, homogeneous attenuation, no focal lesion, no pathologic FDG uptake.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. No focal lesion, no duct dilatation.

Adrenal glands: Bilaterally normal. No adrenal nodule or mass. No adrenal FDG activity above background. (Note: patient on long-term steroid taper/physiologic replacement; adrenal glands appear atrophic but otherwise unremarkable, as expected.)

Kidneys and ureters: Normal bilateral renal size and parenchymal enhancement. Physiologic symmetric renal FDG excretion. No collecting system filling defects. No retroperitoneal adenopathy.

Gastrointestinal tract: Diffuse mild colonic wall FDG activity noted, predominantly in the left and transverse colon, consistent with known prior immune-mediated colitis (now clinically and endoscopically resolved per clinical records). Activity pattern is non-focal and distributed, without discrete mass or focal hypermetabolic lesion. No bowel wall thickening or mass. This pattern is likely residual physiologic/post-inflammatory change and does not suggest active colitis or malignancy. No mesenteric or retroperitoneal adenopathy.

Pelvis: Previously documented right external iliac lymph node (1.2 cm at disease progression, reduced to 0.4 cm on last CT) is no longer measurable as a discrete node and shows no FDG activity. No pelvic adenopathy. No pelvic soft tissue masses. Normal bladder activity (physiologic FDG excretion).

MUSCULOSKELETAL / SOFT TISSUES:
The previously extensive cutaneous and subcutaneous satellite nodules on the trunk, back, abdomen, and bilateral upper extremities (at least 9 discrete hypermetabolic lesions at baseline with SUVmax range 4.1-8.9) are completely resolved. No residual subcutaneous nodularity or abnormal soft tissue FDG uptake in any of these previously involved sites. The body wall soft tissues are unremarkable throughout.

Bony structures: No FDG-avid osseous lesions. No sclerotic or lytic bony lesions on the CT component. No bone marrow FDG activity above normal marrow background. The imaged skeleton appears unremarkable.

MISCELLANEOUS:
Thyroid gland: Mildly heterogeneous in echogenicity on CT, with mildly diffuse increased background FDG activity in the thyroid (SUVmax 3.4), consistent with the known immune-mediated thyroiditis/hypothyroidism on levothyroxine replacement. No discrete hypermetabolic thyroid nodule.

No FDG-avid soft tissue masses or hypermetabolic foci are identified elsewhere in the imaged volume. No new sites of abnormal FDG uptake suggestive of disease involvement.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Markedly impressive and nearly complete metabolic treatment response throughout all previously documented sites of metastatic melanoma, assessed at approximately 12 months from initiation of ipilimumab/nivolumab immunotherapy (currently on nivolumab 480 mg every 4 weeks single-agent maintenance).

2. Right axilla: Tiny residual soft tissue measuring 0.8 cm with only minimal FDG activity (SUVmax 2.1, at the level of mediastinal blood pool reference activity) in the region of the prior 4.8 cm conglomerate nodal mass. This finding is most consistent with fibrotic or necrotic treated residual tissue rather than viable metastatic disease. Comparatively, at baseline this lesion carried an SUVmax of 18.7; the near-complete metabolic quiescence now observed represents an extraordinary response.

3. Liver: No FDG-avid hepatic lesions identified. The minimally perceptible low-level activity in the hepatic segment VII region (SUVmax 1.8) is below the threshold of metabolically active disease and does not exceed normal hepatic parenchymal background. Previously documented segment II, IV, and VI lesions show no CT or FDG correlate.

4. No FDG-avid cervical, axillary, mediastinal, abdominal, or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

5. Complete metabolic resolution of previously extensive cutaneous and subcutaneous satellite nodules.

6. No new hypermetabolic foci suspicious for disease recurrence or progression anywhere in the imaged volume.

7. Mildly increased diffuse thyroid FDG uptake compatible with known immune-mediated thyroiditis; no discrete thyroid lesion.

8. Diffuse mild colonic FDG activity, most consistent with post-inflammatory physiologic change in the setting of prior immune-mediated colitis (clinically resolved); no focal colonic mass or discrete hypermetabolic lesion to suggest active colitis or neoplasm.

Overall, the findings represent a near-complete metabolic response to immunotherapy across all previously documented sites of metastatic BRAF V600E-mutant melanoma. The sole residual finding is a small right axillary soft tissue focus with background-level metabolic activity, most plausibly representing treated fibrotic tissue. No evidence of active or progressive systemic metastatic disease at this time.
